req.title: Run payroll — variant 4
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path monthly payroll run producing correct pay-stub records and audit entry (EP), (2) concurrent-run conflict returning 409 (NEG/STC), (3) atomic rollback on mid-run failure (NEG/DT), (4) rate-limit enforcement after N rapid requests (BVA), and (5) state-machine transition from idle→running→completed (STC). Tests are scoped to the /api/payroll-runs REST endpoint with an hr-admin token, using pytest fixtures for auth and data setup. Each test tears down its own state via the <cleanup_payroll_run> helper to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_audit
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
At least one active employee exists (seeded by <seed_active_employees> fixture)
No payroll run currently in-progress for the period
actions: Poll or await until run state == completed
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with run_id and state=queued or running
Final GET on run returns state=completed
Pay-stub records exist for every active employee with gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains exactly one entry referencing run_id with action=payroll_run_completed
trace: R-WEB-075: gross pay, deductions, net pay computed per active employee
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_payroll_run_happy_path_creates_stubs_and_audit(
    hr_admin_token, seed_active_employees, api_base_url, cleanup_payroll_run
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    payload = {"period": "2024-05", "type": "monthly"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]
    cleanup_payroll_run(run_id)

    run = poll_until(lambda: requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}", headers=headers
    ).json(), condition=lambda r: r["state"] == "completed", timeout=30)
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/stubs", headers=headers).json()
    assert len(stubs) >= 1
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"])

    audit = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}", headers=headers).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit["entries"]]
    assert "payroll_run_completed" in actions

tests.name: test_payroll_run_concurrent_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
A payroll run for the same period is already in-progress (seeded by <seed_inprogress_run> fixture)
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with the same period while a run is already in-progress
expected: Response body contains error code run_in_progress or equivalent
No new run record is created
trace: R-WEB-075: 409 if another run is in progress
R-WEB-075: state transition coverage — idle state not reachable while run active
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_payroll_run_concurrent_conflict_returns_409(
    hr_admin_token, seed_inprogress_run, api_base_url
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    payload = {"period": seed_inprogress_run["period"], "type": "monthly"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409

    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    error_code = body["error"].get("code", body["error"])
    assert "run_in_progress" in str(error_code).lower() or resp.status_code == 409

    runs = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs?period={seed_inprogress_run['period']}",
        headers=headers
    ).json()
    in_progress = [r for r in runs["items"] if r["state"] == "running"]
    assert len(in_progress) == 1, "Only the original run should exist"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_failure_rolls_back_atomically
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Fault-injection is enabled via <enable_payroll_fault_injection> fixture so mid-run processing fails
No prior stubs exist for the test period
actions: POST /api/payroll-runs with a period targeting the fault-injected scenario
Poll run until state is failed or error
GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/stubs to verify no partial records
expected: HTTP 500 or run.error_code indicates internal failure
Zero pay-stub records exist for the failed run (full rollback)
Audit log records failure event, not completion
trace: R-WEB-075: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-075: failed run shall not partially commit
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_payroll_run_failure_rolls_back_atomically(
    hr_admin_token, enable_payroll_fault_injection, api_base_url, cleanup_payroll_run
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    payload = {"period": "2024-06", "type": "monthly"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 202)
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]
    cleanup_payroll_run(run_id)

    run = poll_until(
        lambda: requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}", headers=headers).json(),
        condition=lambda r: r["state"] in ("failed", "error"),
        timeout=30
    )
    assert run["state"] in ("failed", "error")

    stubs_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/stubs", headers=headers
    )
    stubs = stubs_resp.json().get("items", [])
    assert len(stubs) == 0, "Partial stubs must not persist after rollback"

    audit = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}", headers=headers).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit["entries"]]
    assert any("fail" in a.lower() or "rollback" in a.lower() for a in actions)

tests.name: test_payroll_run_rate_limit_at_threshold_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Rate-limit window is reset via <reset_rate_limit> fixture
Rate limit for hr-admin on POST /api/payroll-runs is N requests per window (N obtained from <rate_limit_config> fixture)
actions: Send exactly N POST requests to /api/payroll-runs in rapid succession
expected: First N responses return 201 or 409 (valid business responses, not rate-limited)
N+1-th response returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
trace: R-WEB-075: operation must be rate-limited per user
BVA: boundary at exactly N (allowed) and N+1 (rejected)

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transitions_idle_to_completed
preconditions: Authenticated hr-admin token available via <hr_admin_token> fixture
No active run for the test period
At least one active employee exists
actions: Await final state and observe completed state
expected: Initial state is queued or running (not completed immediately)
Intermediate poll returns state=running at least once
trace: STC: idle -> queued -> running -> completed transition path
tests.executable:
import requests
import time

def test_payroll_run_state_transitions_idle_to_completed(
    hr_admin_token, seed_active_employees, api_base_url, cleanup_payroll_run
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    payload = {"period": "2024-08", "type": "monthly"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 202)
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]
    cleanup_payroll_run(run_id)

    observed_states = []
    deadline = time.time() + 45
    prev_state = None
    while time.time() < deadline:
        run = requests.get(
            f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}", headers=headers
        ).json()
        state = run["state"]
        observed_states.append(state)
        if prev_state == "completed":
            assert state == "completed", "State must not regress from completed"
        prev_state = state
        if state == "completed":
            break
        time.sleep(2)

    assert "completed" in observed_states, f"Run never completed: {observed_states}"
    valid_order = ["queued", "running", "completed"]
    filtered = [s for s in observed_states if s in valid_order]
    assert filtered == sorted(filtered, key=valid_order.index)
